info.name: Campaign Intelligence Engine
info.description:
API collection for the Resulticks Campaign Performance Intelligence Engine.

Base URL : http://127.0.0.1:8001
LLM      : Ollama qwen2.5:14b via Resulticks proxy (10.102.1.2:7557)
Trained  : tenant 00b4e220_6121_4a93_a63f_d0848bd73506 (short-code hUe)

Workflow:
  1. POST /generate-from-campaign  ← full auto pipeline (recommended)
  2. POST /ml/score                ← inspect ML scores only
  3. POST /generate-ollama         ← LLM with hand-crafted payload

request.description: Returns {"status": "ok", "version": "..."} when the server is running.
Returns service name, version, and available endpoint list.
Open in a browser to explore all endpoints interactively.

item.name: 2. DB-Driven Pipeline (generate-from-campaign)
item.description:
Full auto pipeline: campaign_id + tenant_short_code → DB fetch → ML scoring → Ollama LLM → structured insights.

Requires:
- Resulticks ProxySQL access (10.200.2.195:6033)
- Ollama proxy access (10.102.1.2:7557)

PostgreSQL dedup/persist is optional (skipped gracefully if PG is not running).

request.description:
Verified working example.

campaign_id         : integer from ccampaign.CampaignID (per-tenant fact table)
tenant_short_code   : TenantShortCode from resulticksmaster.TenantLookup (case-sensitive)
bu_id               : numeric BU ID (0 = default BU)
segmentation_list_id: SegmentationListID that filters the fact table and historical context

The engine:
1. Resolves tenant UUID from short-code
2. Fetches campaign metadata + metrics from ccampaign / ccampaignchannelmapping
3. Fetches last-10-campaign historical avg per channel from per-tenant MySQL
4. Runs 4-tier ML scoring (BU → Tenant → Market → Industry fallback)
5. Calls Ollama qwen2.5:14b for 5-10 structured insights
6. Deduplicates against 30-day history (if PostgreSQL available)

response.name: 200 — Insights generated
response.body:
{
  "campaign_id": "40182",
  "llm_model": "qwen2.5:14b",
  "confidence_avg": 78.4,
  "insights": [
    {
      "insight_type": "Performance",
      "category": "Benchmark",
      "scope": "BU",
      "title": "Email Open Rate Below BU Average",
      "observation": "Email open rate was 14.2%, against BU average of 24.8% — a deficit of 10.6 percentage points.",
      "root_cause": "engagement_quality_score of 0.31 and timing_quality_score of 0.44 suggest suboptimal send-time and subject-line fatigue.",
      "recommendation": "A/B test subject lines with personalised tokens by 15 Aug; shift send window from 09:00 to 11:00–13:00 IST based on BU peak open data.",
      "business_impact": "Closing the 10.6pp gap on a 50 000-recipient list adds ~5 300 opens and an estimated +1.8pp conversion uplift.",
      "confidence": 82
    }
  ],
  "ml_scores": {
    "reach_score": 0.43,
    "engagement_quality_score": 0.31,
    "channel_efficiency_email": 0.29,
    "audience_fit_score": 0.67
  },
  "generated_at": "2026-07-10T08:30:00.000Z"
}

response.name: 502 — DB unreachable
response.body: {"detail": "Resulticks DB fetch failed: Can't connect to MySQL server on '10.200.2.195'"}

response.name: 503 — LLM unavailable
response.body: {"detail": "LLM service error: Cannot reach Ollama endpoint at http://10.102.1.2:7557"}

request.description: Replace campaign_id with any real CampaignID from the tenant's ccampaign table.

item.name: Get Stored Insights — By Campaign ID

request.description:
Retrieve previously generated and persisted insights from PostgreSQL.

Returns 404 if PostgreSQL is not running or no insights were stored for this campaign_id.

item.name: 3. Ollama LLM — Manual Payload (generate-ollama)
item.description: Call the LLM directly with pre-computed metrics and (optionally) ML scores. Useful for testing prompt quality or injecting custom data without touching the DB.

request.description:
Minimal payload — email channel only, no ML scores. The LLM derives insights from raw metrics alone.

Required fields: tenant_id, bu_id, campaign_name, segment_size, audience_reach, email_sent.
All ML score fields are optional (omit or set to null).

item.name: Generate Insights — All Channels + ML Scores (Full)

request.description:
Full payload: email + SMS + WhatsApp, all 13 ML scores, benchmark deltas, anomaly flags.

ML scores are 0–1 normalized. Providing them gives the LLM richer signal and produces higher-quality insights.

Insight quality order:
  Full payload > Partial scores > Raw metrics only

item.name: 4. Manual Insight Generation (generate)
item.description: Full pipeline with a manually constructed payload — hierarchy, metadata, metrics, audience features, and historical context all provided explicitly. Useful for testing or when the campaign data is not in the Resulticks DB.

request.description:
Fully manual payload — all hierarchy, metadata, metrics, audience, and historical context provided explicitly.

Use this when: you want to test the pipeline with synthetic data, or the campaign is not in the Resulticks DB.

item.name: 5. ML Scoring
item.description: Score a campaign across all 13 ML dimensions without calling the LLM. Use to verify model output before generating insights, or to build dashboards.

request.description:
Returns all 13 ML scores (0–1 normalised), scope_used, anomaly_flags, and model_confidence.

campaign_type_code: M=Promotional, S=Triggered, T=Transactional

Paste the returned scores into the generate-ollama body for richest insight quality.
Minimal scoring — email only, Transactional type. Rates auto-derived from counts when not provided.
Small triggered/event-based campaign. High open rate expected. campaign_type_code=S.

item.name: 6. ML Predict
item.description: Direct XGBoost/LightGBM prediction — returns raw model outputs and normalised score quantiles per target dimension. Lower-level than /score.

request.description: Returns raw per-target predictions + normalised quantile scores. campaign_type_code: M=Promotional, S=Triggered, T=Transactional.
Email + SMS together.

item.name: 7. ML Models
item.description: List trained model bundles and inspect per-scope metadata.

request.description: Returns all .joblib bundles found in ml/trained_models/. Each entry shows scope, scope_id, target, n_samples, val_mae, val_r2, trained_at.

scope_id format for BU: {tenant_uuid}_{bu_id}
Example: 00b4e220_6121_4a93_a63f_d0848bd73506_0
scope_id = tenant UUID.
Market-level XGBoost model. scope_id = market identifier string.
Industry-level LightGBM fallback model. scope_id is typically 'global' or an industry code.
Clears the in-memory model cache. Run immediately after retraining new .joblib bundles so the next request loads the fresh models.

item.name: 8. Benchmarks
item.description: Retrieve benchmark profiles for a scope. Used by the ML scorer to compute delta_vs_bu, delta_vs_industry, and percentile_rank.

request.description: Benchmark profile for the trained tenant (UUID 00b4e220_6121_4a93_a63f_d0848bd73506, short-code hUe).

item.name: 9. End-to-End Workflow
item.description: Two-step workflow: score first, then paste ML scores into generate-ollama for the richest possible insight quality.

request.description:
WORKFLOW STEP 1 of 2.

Run this first. Copy the returned scores (reach_score, engagement_quality_score, …) into Step 2's body.

item.name: Step 2 — Generate Insights with ML Scores
